item.title: Trails in the Sky 2nd Chapter launches September 17
item.content:encoded:
In the west, Trails in the Sky 2nd Chapter will be available both physically and digitally for PlayStation 5, Switch 2, and Switch. In Japan, the physical edition will only be available for PlayStation 5 and Switch 2.

In Japan, pre-orders for Trails in the Sky 2nd Chapter on PlayStation 5, Switch 2, or Switch will include a download code for a high-definition remastered version of the original The Legend of Heroes: Trails in the Sky.

Get the latest details below.

■ Uroboros Box (Japan) (13,860 yen)

The Trails in the Sky 2nd Chapter Uroboros Box and Trails in the Sky 2nd Chapter – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition Uroboros Box includes include numerous exclusive items, all housed in a special, custom-designed case inspired by the Uroboros organization.

(1) A copy of Trails in the Sky 2nd Chapter for PlayStation 5, Trails in the Sky 2nd Chapter – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition, or a Steam key code for Trails in the Sky 2nd Chapter

(2) Trails in the Sky Memorial Sound Collection

A commemorative album featuring carefully selected arranged tracks from the Trails in the Sky series, including the Trails in the Sky 2nd Chapter arrangements of background music and the theme song “Silver Will, Golden Wings.”

(3) Downloadable Content Costume: “Fluffy Sheep Mascot Set”

Limited edition downloadable content allowing all party members, including Estelle and Joshua, to wear fluffy “sheep-style” mascot costumes.

(4) Blu-ray: The Legend of Heroes: Trails in the Sky THE ANIMATION – Timeless Edition

The Legend of Heroes: Trails in the Sky THE ANIMATION, the original animation produced between 2011 and 2012, is now available as a Blu-ray set with subtitles in multiple languages. This “Timeless Edition” includes all of the original bonus footage and audio commentary. (Subtitle languages include English, Japanese, Korean, Traditional Chinese, and Simplified Chinese.)

(5) Liberl Kingdom: Complete Character Book

A comprehensive character encyclopedia with information on every citizen of Liberl Kingdom featured in Trails in the Sky 1st Chapter and Trails in the Sky 2nd Chapter. Roughly 500 characters are detailed, including the NPCs from each region.

This book offers perhaps everything there is to know about the people living in Liberl, and reading it while you play enables a deeper appreciation of the world.

■ Pre-Order Bonus (Japan) (physical and digital)

The Legend of Heroes: Trails in the Sky Download Code

A code to download The Legend of Heroes: Trails in the Sky, the game that served as the basis for the remake Trails in the Sky 1st Chapter. In addition to it being a high-definition remaster, it also offers quality-of-life features like a high-speed mode and text log.

(This bonus is not available for the Steam version.)

■ Trails in the Sky 1st Chapter Clear Data Bonus

“Classic Style SC” Costumes for Estelle and Joshua

Players with Trails in the Sky 1st Chapter clear data can unlock special costumes for Estelle and Joshua in Trails in the Sky 2nd Chapter, which are based on their original outfits from The Legend of Heroes: Trails in the Sky SC.

■ Characters

Anelace Elfead (voiced Cassandra Lee Morris in English, Sora Tokui in Japanese)

  • Age: 18
  • Weapon: One-handed sword

A recently promoted senior bracer and Estelle’s senior. She has a major soft spot for anything cute.

Despite being very driven and diligent, Anelace has a refreshingly straightforward personality and tends not to sweat the details.

Anelace is the granddaughter of Yun Ka-fai, the founder of the Eastern art Eight Leaves One Blade. Though aware she’s still lacking in experience, Anelace aspires to live up to her grandfather’s legacy and master the art of the sword herself one day.

Kevin Graham (voiced Ray Chase in English, Kazuyuki Okitsu in Japanese)

  • Age: 21
  • Weapon: Bow gun

A wandering priest of the Septian Church, recognizable for his distinctive accent and demeanor.

With his friendly, easy-going personality, Kevin may not seem like the most orthodox of priests. But beneath his light-hearted humor lies a compassionate and thoughtful individual.

Though the bulk of his work consists of traveling to remote villages to deliver sermons and run Sunday School for local children, his true purpose for being in Liberl seems to lie elsewhere…

Major Mueller Vander (voiced by Patrick Seitz in English, Ryuichi Kijima in Japanese)

  • Age: 28
  • Weapon: Greatsword

An imperial soldier dispatched to the Liberl Kingdom, currently stationed in the Erebonian Embassy as a military attaché.

Mueller is a childhood friend of Olivier, who is also from the Empire. Olivier’s eccentricities are a constant source of headaches for Mueller, but the pair’s seamless rapport suggests a deep bond of trust.

A skilled practitioner of the Empire’s renowned Vander swordsmanship school, Mueller deftly wields his heavy greatsword with a single hand. His strength is said to already be close to that of a master.

Captain Julia Schwarz (voiced by Gabriella Certo in English, Eriko Matsui in Japanese)

  • Age: 27
  • Weapon: Rapier

The commander of Queen Alicia’s Royal Guard and captain of the Arseille, a cutting-edge airship.

Promoted to Captain for her heroism during the coup incident that threatened the kingdom, Julia has been spending her days running test flights for the Arseille’s new engine.

Her sincere demeanor and dignified aura have earned her quite a bit of popularity among both citizens and fellow soldiers.

Leonhardt (voiced Stephen Fu in English, Satoshi Hino in Japanese)

  • Age: 26

Enforcer No. II of the Society of Ouroboros. His inordinately formidable skills as a swordman have earned him the name “Bladelord.”

Though Leonhardt’s outward demeanor is calm and dispassionate, he harbors a deep rage below the surface, intense enough to drive him to murderous violence.

In the course of his endeavors to do the Society’s bidding across the kingdom, he has already crossed paths with Estelle and her allies under the guise of “Lieutenant Lorence” many times.

Weissmann, the Faceless (voiced Liam O’Brien in English, Nobuo Tobita in Japanese)

  • Age: 37

The leader linking the seven Enforcers of Ouroboros, known by the alias “The Faceless”.

A twisted, corrupt individual, Weissmann uses a supernatural ability known as “Absolute Suggestion” to alter people’s memories and perceptions at will. With this power, he was able to manipulate the events of 1st Chapter from the shadows.

For the sake of the Society’s mysterious plan, following the wishes of the Grandmaster, he shepherds the Enforcers in a plot to bring calamity to Liberl.

Renne (voiced by Courtney Lin in English, Aoi Yuuki in Japanese)

  • Age: 11

A young girl from Crossbell State who has come to Liberl on vacation with her merchant parents.

Precocious yet fickle as a kitten, Renne has a mischievous personality. Her innocence and naivety can often force the adults around her into difficult situations.

Though she has been enjoying her sightseeing around the kingdom, a chance encounter leads Renne to begin accompanying Estelle on her journey.

item.title: Telltale Brings Five-Chapter Sci-Fi Adventure 'The Expanse' To Switch
item.content:encoded:
A story-driven prequel to the popular TV show

The famous narrative game studio Telltale, known for series such as Sam & Max, has this week surprised fans with the release of The Expanse: A Telltale Series Deluxe Edition on the Switch eShop.

This story-driven sci-fi adventure, developed in partnership with Deck Nine (Life is Strange), is set before the events of The Expanse TV show and comes with five main chapters. The Deluxe Edition also includes a bonus episode.

Here's some additional information, along with some screenshots of the Switch release:

"Experience the exciting universe of The Expanse like never before in Telltale’s latest adventure, The Expanse: A Telltale Series. Take on the role of Camina Drummer and explore the dangerous and uncharted edges of the Belt aboard the Artemis. From scavenging wrecked ships in a zero-g environment, to surviving a mutiny, to combating fearsome pirates, you make the difficult choices and reveal Camina Drummer’s resolve in this latest Telltale adventure."

"Contend with the unforgiving brutality of space, the politics of a corrupt solar system, the animosities of your crew, and Drummer’s own conflicting responsibilities to the people she cares about and the Belt she has sworn to protect. There is no right or wrong path - only your choices and the consequences they unleash."

THE DELUXE EDITION INCLUDES
Episode 1: Archer's Paradox
Episode 2: Hunting Grounds
Episode 3: First Ones
Episode 4: Impossible Objects
Episode 5: Europa's Folly
Bonus Episode: ARCHANGEL

The Expanse: A Telltale Series Deluxe Edition is now available on the Switch eShop for $29.99 USD (or your regional equivalent).

Would you be interested in playing this on Switch? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Bluesky

item.title: Granblue Fantasy: Relink – Endless Ragnarok open beta test set for April 24 to 27
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS4
  • PS5
  • Switch 2

Call upon a multitude of mighty powers to change the tide of battle.

Publisher Cygames and developer Cygames Osaka will host an open beta test for Granblue Fantasy: Relink – Endless Ragnarok from April 24 to 27, the companies announced.

Here are the details:

■ About the Open Beta

This beta is intended to let players experience the new Summon mechanic while helping us verify network performance, game functionality, and identify and resolve in-game issues prior to launch. We’ve also prepared a short survey for all participants. Please take a moment to share your thoughts after playing—your feedback is greatly appreciated!

As this game’s development is still in progress, its content may differ from the retail version. During the open beta test, you may encounter bugs, connection issues, freezing, or crashes. Thank you for bearing with us!

How to Participate

This beta is open to all players on our supported platforms. Simply download the game from your respective platform’s store and launch the game once the beta begins!

  • PlayStation 5
  • Switch 2
  • PlayStation 4
  • PC (Steam)

Preloading begins on April 23, 2026 at 7:00 a.m. UTC.

Open beta begins on April 24, 2026 at 7:00 a.m. UTC.

*If you already have the closed beta application installed, you can participate by installing the open beta update.
*A Nintendo Switch Online membership is required to participate on Switch 2.
*A PlayStation Plus subscription is not required to participate on PlayStation 5 or PlayStation 4.
*The Steam version requires the installation of the Steam client.

Playable Content

  • Tutorial Mode
  • Quest Mode (four available)

*Only Tutorial Mode and Quest Mode are playable during the open beta.
*Online multiplayer is available for all quests in Quest Mode.
*Local wireless play for Switch 2 is available during the open beta.
*Quest Mode contains the same three quests available during the closed beta.
*The new Summon mechanic can be unlocked upon completing the three quests, and can be used in the newly added fourth quest.
*If you participated in the closed beta test, quest completion progress from preexisting save data will be carried over to the open beta test. Gear and party composition will not be carried over.

New Feature: Summons

Summons allow players to call upon a multitude of mighty powers to change the tide of battle—and now they’re yours to wield in the open beta.

Using Summons:

  • First, equip summons from the main menu to use them in battle.
  • Then, while questing, use link attacks or Skybound Arts to fill the summon gauge, which is shared by the whole party.
  • Once filled, call your summon to wreak devastation with your favorite civilian, monstrous, or primal allies!

Primal Burst:

  • Entering link time will cause Lyria to begin channeling a primal burst.
  • Afterward, when all members of the party use their Skybound Arts to trigger a full burst, Lyria will follow up with the primal burst.


*Six summons are available to use in the open beta test.
*As this game is still in development, their properties may differ from the retail version.
*Quests where summons can be used are labeled “Summons Allowed” on the quest overview. In the open beta, summons are only available in “Sephira’s Sanguine Glimmer.”
*The “Sephira’s Sanguine Glimmer” quest is unlocked after completing the other three quests.
*Screenshots taken from the PlayStation 5 version.

Playable Characters

  • Main Character
  • Katalina
  • Rackam
  • Io
  • Charlotta
  • Ghandagoza
  • Narmaya
  • Lancelot
  • Siegfried
  • Yodarha
  • Zeta
  • Beatrix

Local Wireless Play (Switch 2 Only)

Local wireless play for Switch 2 is available during the open beta.

*Please note that this title does not support GameShare on the Switch 2, and must be downloaded on each console individually.
*Additional games, systems, and/or accessories may be required for multiplayer mode.

■ Open Beta Overview

Schedule

  • Start: April 24, 2026 at 7:00 a.m. UTC
  • End: April 27, 2026 at 6:59 a.m. UTC

*If any issues arise during the open beta, the schedule may be affected by emergency maintenance.
*Updates will be posted on the official Granblue Fantasy: Relink website and social media channels.

Eligibility

  • Be 18 years or older.
  • Have a stable broadband Internet connection.
  • Be able to complete a web survey after participating in the open beta.
  • Have read and consent to the Disclaimer, Terms of Service, and Privacy Policy.
  • Switch 2 Participants: A Nintendo Switch Online membership is required to access multiplayer during the open beta.
  • Have a Switch 2, PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, or PC that meets the system requirements listed below.

Supported Platforms

  • PlayStation 5
  • Switch 2
  • PlayStation 4
  • PC (Steam)

Supported Languages (Interface)

  • English
  • Japanese
  • French
  • Italian
  • German
  • Spanish
  • Brazilian Portuguese
  • Traditional Chinese
  • Simplified Chinese
  • Korean

Supported Languages (Audio)

  • English
  • Japanese

System Requirements

Steam open beta test system requirements:

MinimumRecommended
OSWindows 11 (64-bit Required)Windows 11 (64-bit Required)
ProcessorIntel Core i3-9100 / AMD Ryzen 3 3200GIntel Core i7-8700 / AMD Ryzen 5 3600
Memory16 GB RAM16 GB RAM
GraphicsNVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 6GB / AMD Radeon RX 580 8GBNVIDIA GeForce RTX 2080 8GB / AMD Radeon RX 6700 XT 8GB
Storage34 GB available space34 GB available space
Additional NotesSSD recommended (Capable of 1080p/30fps when graphics are set to Standard.)SSD recommended (Capable of 1080p/60fps when graphics are set to Ultra.)

*These specifications apply specifically to the open beta version of Granblue Fantasy: Relink – Endless Ragnarok.
*Minimum and recommended system requirements for the open beta are subject to change without notice.
*System requirements for the open beta may differ from those of the retail version of the game.
*At least 10 gigabytes of available storage space is required to play the game on the Switch 2.
*At least 21 gigabytes of available storage space is required to play the game on the PlayStation 5.
*At least 11 gigabytes of available storage space is required to play the game on the PlayStation 4.

Granblue Fantasy: Relink – Endless Ragnarok is due out for PlayStation 5, Switch 2, PlayStation 4, and PC via Steam on July 9.

Watch a new trailer below.

Introductory Movie

English

Japanese

item.title: The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales details Age of Magic
item.content:encoded:
Get the details below.

■ Story

A Story of Four Ages across a Thousand Years

To lift the curse on Princess Heuria of Huther, Elliot and Faie will cross through the Doorway of Time to visit different periods in history.

They will travel from their own time, the Age of Safekeeping, further and further into the past. Across every age, a terrible threat looms over Elliot and Faie’s adventure.

—Age of Safekeeping: The Present

(Read more here.)

—Age of Reconstruction: The Dark Ages

(Read more here.)

—Age of Magic: A Golden Age

(Today’s update.)

—Age of Budding: The Birth of Civilization

(To be detailed at a later date.)

■ Age of Magic

Further back in time than the Age of Reconstruction is the Age of Magic. It is the height of prosperity for humanity, driven by technological advances in magic—so much so as to even drive back the beast tribes.

But the future beyond this prosperity is a world of ruin. Doubtless, somewhere in this age, gears are out of alignment.

Weyzn

Weyzn is a nation of magic lined with magnificent buildings, quite out of place in the beastmen—filled Philabieldia familiar to Elliot. The nation’s prosperity is supported by two magical research institutions: Forthewor, which doggedly pursues technological advancement by any means, and Formanit, which follows the enrichment of people’s lives as its lodestar. The lives of the citizens of Weyzn are wholly dependent on magic, and research on it continues unceasingly.

■ Characters

Fausta (voiced by Elizabeth Maxwell in English, Sayaka Oohara in Japanese)

Formerly of Forthewor, this erudite researcher went independent to form and head the Formanit Institute.

Her earnest and rigorous approach to research has earned her the profound trust of her employees.

Marnie (voiced by Suzie Yeung, Sayumi Suzushiro in Japanese)

A researcher at the Formanit Institute. She is an expert in magic technology, particularly in the field of mechanical engineering, and carries out field tests of the magical organisms known as thaumata.

Marnie looks up to Director Fausta and devotes herself wholly to the institute’s principle of magic research to make people happy.

Hildebrandt (voiced by Christopher Smith in English, Show Hayami in Japanese)

Director of the Forthewor Institute. A realist who prioritizes results, the numerous fruits of his outstanding research skills saw him appointed as director at a young age. He is regarded highly by the populace of Weyzn for his breakthroughs in magic development.

Ikarus (voiced by Chris Okawa in English, Yuuya Uchida in Japanese)

Head of Weyzn’s nobility. Stern and serious, he spares no effort in fulfilling his responsibility of governing Weyzn. Due to his concerns about Forthewor’s reckless pursuit of progress, he has formed a cooperative relationship with Fausta, to whom he turns for advice.

■ System: Weapon Enhancements

Elliot can find seven types of weapons to support him on his adventure. Stronger versions exist among weapons of the same type, boasting not simply greater attack power, but stronger special attacks as well. Find the weapons scattered throughout the caves and ruins of the various ages and stay on the leading edge of adventuring.

—Some more powerful weapons lie in areas unrelated to the main story. If you come across someplace yet unexplored, a detour may be in order.

—With a stronger weapon, the possibilities expand for how it might be combined with other weapons or customized with magicite. Put some thought into new battle strategies whenever you acquire a new weapon.

■ System: Magicite Enhancements

Magicite, which imparts various effects to weapons, comes in different rarities. Even among magicite with the same effect, rarer versions may boast greater effects. The more magicite you form, the greater chances you have of forming rarer magicite, so form early and form often.

—Lightning mallet magicite adds bolts of lightning to hammer special attacks. Rarer versions add more bolts, striking enemies in a wider area.

—There is a limit to how much magicite you can equip, with more powerful magicite coming at a greater cost. As rarity goes up, however, down goes the cost, allowing you to enjoy even more flexible customization.

■ Adventuring Diversion: Faie’s Magic Lessons

Finding new fairy magic for Faie unlocks minigames in the form of Faie’s magic lessons. Make the most of a newly learned magic ability to earn points in that particular lesson. Based on your score when clearing the lesson, you’ll be rewarded with records that allow you to listen to the in – game soundtrack.

—Access Faie’s magic lessons from her part of the menu.

—Listen to records in the Music menu. In addition to score-based rewards, some records can also be picked up mid-minigame.

Magic Lesson: Sprint

Race along the snowy slope while racking up points. Reach the goal before the avalanche reaches you for an even higher score.

Magic Lesson: Warp

Warp around from platform to platform in this lava-filled stage. Watch out for platforms that move and sink, and walls that are a challenge to warp through.

item.title: Lana Del Rey Releases 007 First Light Official Theme Song
item.content:encoded:
Are you ready?

The talented American singer and songwriter Lana Del Rey has today released her theme song for 007 First Light.

As highlighted by Rolling Stone, First Light is a "grand and orchestral affair, embellished with motifs from the James Bond theme song". The track has been composed and co-written by David Arnold, who has previously scored 007 movies such as Casino Royale and Tomorrow Never Dies.

David Arnold: "Having been part of that musical lineage, I understand how important it is to honor its heritage while allowing it to evolve. Lana brought an elegance and atmosphere to the song that felt perfectly suited to continuing that tradition while introducing something fresh for a new era.”

The theme song announcement follows news earlier this month that 007 First Light had been delayed for the Switch 2. Instead of launching on 27th May 2026 alongside other platforms, the Nintendo version is now scheduled for "later this summer". Earlier this week, spoilers related to First Light also reportedly surfaced in a classification board leak.

Apart from Lana's involvement in the theme song, the singer and musician Lenny Kravitz will appear in 007 First Light as a black-market dealer named Bawma.

So, what do you think of the theme song for 007 First Light? Let us know in the comments.

[source rollingstone.com]

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

item.title: NYT Strands #774 Hints and Answers for Today, April 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #774 for Thursday, April 16, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, April 16, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #774 are all related to terms you use to describe your hobbies

General Strands Hints For April 16

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: The “rec” in rec room.
  • Hint 2: Baseball, the great American ____.
  • Hint 3: When you have interests or skills that you do for leisure.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #774 for April 16, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for April 16, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • FORE
  • TASER
  • JUST
  • PAST
  • TIME

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #774 On April 16, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • PASTIME
  • INTEREST
  • RECREATION
  • HOBBY
  • SIDELINE
  • SPANAGRAM: JUSTFORFUN

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #774?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on April 17 will be Strands Game #775.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Puzzle Bobble Everybubble! coming to PC on April 23
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Currently available for Switch.

Publisher Arc System Works and developer TAITO will release Puzzle Bobble Everybubble! for PC via Steam on April 23 for $39.99, the companies announced.

Puzzle Bobble Everybubble! first launched for Switch on May 23, 2023.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

Play solo! Play with friends! The original bubble shooter is back!

The latest entry in the iconic action-puzzle series Puzzle Bobble, also known as Bust-A-Move, is finally on Steam! Join forces with Bub and his friends to clear the bubbles covering Rainbow Island and restore peace to the land.

Key Features

  • Co-Op Story Mode – For the first time in the series, team up with up to 4 players for local cooperative play!
  • More Content Than Ever – Experience a charming world with quirky characters and a substantial story—the most content-packed title in the series to date!
  • Global Rankings – Compete for high scores with players from around the world and climb the online leaderboards.
  • Two-Versus-Two Team Battles – A series first! Face off in intense 2vs2 team matches in local play.
  • Online Duels – Test your skills against players worldwide in 1-vs-1 online matches.
  • Play online matches with friends far away using the Password Matchmaking feature!
  • Includes the Space Invaders 45th Anniversary collaboration mini-game, Puzzle Bobble vs. Space Invaders!

Watch a new trailer below.

PC Announce Trailer

item.title: Spy Drops coming to PS5, Xbox Series on April 28
item.content:encoded:
• PS5
  • Xbox Series

Currently available for Switch and PC.

Publisher Rainy Frog and developer Rainy Night Creations will release “modern retro stealth action” game Spy Drops for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series on April 28, the companies announced.

Spy Drops first launched for Switch and PC via Steam on July 31, 2025.

Here is an overview of the game, via Rainy Frog:

Experience retro 3D stealth action with modern effects, randomly generated missions, modern equipment, and local multiplayer!

As a member of the counter terrorist Spy Drops team, you are tasked with neutralizing a threat that could plunge the world into digital darkness, while you unknowingly get tangled in a deeper conspiracy.

Dropped behind enemy lines, your covert ops are to infiltrate infrastructure and undertake secret ops. While onsite you also need to make use of the Dream Catcher to extract intel from the minds of soldiers to discover the location of the next targets.

Missions are randomly generated producing a tense gameplay experience as you never know what is around the next corner. Everyone’s game will be different.

You’ll explore enemy bases room-by-room, choosing how to tackle each one, navigating past enemies who can see your shadow and hear your footsteps. You can dodge, hack or shot cameras, sneak past or knock out guards, scout the area with a drone, hide in lockers, or even wear sponge boots to move around quieter.

You’re free to choose which equipment you take on each mission, choosing from drones, hacking devices, explosives, night vision googles and many more. Varying times of day and weather conditions can be a hindrance or provide cover, so you’ll need to equip accordingly. New equipment and weapons are researched and added as the game progresses.

Take on a friend in local head-to-head multiplayer with Spy against Spy!

Jump into random missions outside the main story and push your skills to the limit. Your times in this mode will upload to the global leaderboards, where you can battle for the top spot against players all over the world.

Watch a new trailer below.

PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/spy-drops-coming-to-ps5-xbox-series-on-april-28

item.title: Epic Games’ Chief People Officer Gone From Company
item.content:encoded:
Monika Fahlbusch has parted ways with Epic Games. Labeled the “Chief People Officer,” Fahlbusch was with the company for just over 5 years, starting in December 2020. According to a new report, Epic has confirmed her departure.

As a top executive in the HR department, Fahlbusch reportedly played a role in the latest round of cuts, which saw the Fortnite publisher lay off more than 1,000 people in the latter half of March 2026. In the wake of the cuts, Fahlbusch sent a letter to the North Carolina Department of Commerce where she stated on behalf of the company, “We are mindful of the difficulties that this layoff poses to employees and the community.”

Epic Games Confirms Departure of HR Exec Monika Fahlbusch

According to a Kotaku report, Epic Games confirmed that Monika Fahlbusch’s last day with the company was April 15. However, the company ultimately declined to elaborate on the reason she was exiting her role.

Her previous work experience before joining Epic included two years in a similar role for JUUL Labs. Experience notes that she has over 30 years of experience leading “People functions.” Her additional experience includes roles at Salesforce and BMC Software.

More on the Recent Epic Layoffs

When Epic Games announced it was laying off more than 1,000 employees in March 2026, the difficult decision was blamed on lower-than-expected engagement in Fortnite and other areas of the games business. Unfortunately, one of the individuals laid off was programmer-writer Mike Prinke, an individual battling terminal brain cancer.

Following the cuts, his wife shared on social media that they were concerned about him losing the life insurance he received as an employee benefit at Epic. In the wake of that, Epic CEO Tim Sweeney issued a statement apologizing for not recognizing the situation in advance and claimed that Prinke’s medical issue was not a factor in the decision to lay him off. Learn more about that at this link.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor this story and report any updates.

What are your thoughts on the departure of this Epic Games HR executive? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Diablo 4 Lord of Hatred DLC Rated for Nintendo Switch in Indonesia
item.content:encoded:
Diablo 4’s Lord of Hatred DLC has apparently been rated by Indonesia’s ratings board for a new console. Recently, the API for the Indonesia Game Rating System (IGRS) revealed a rating for the Diablo 4 Lord of Hatred DLC specifically for the Nintendo Switch.

Specifically, the content is rated for the original Nintendo Switch, as well as for PC, Sony PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series consoles.

The IGRS’ Rating for Diablo 4: Lord of Hatred Explained

The Indonesia Game Ratings System rated the forthcoming Diablo 4 expansion 18+. 18+ is the second-most mature rating the board gives. The next rating underneath it is RC (refused classification). That rating is reserved for games that contain prohibited content akin to what would land a game with an AO rating in the ESRB.

The full descriptor for the 18+ rating can be seen below:

“Game content suitable for ages 18 and over (18+) does not contain pornography, but may display references to cigarettes, alcohol, or drugs; animated violence, blood, mutilation, and cannibalism; adult humor with sexual connotations; human-like characters without showing genitals, breasts, or buttocks; gambling activities that do not involve real or digital currency; horror elements intended to provoke fear; and online conversation features.”

More on Diablo 4: Lord of Hatred

The major expansion for Diablo 4, coming on April 28, is slated to introduce two new classes for players to use. One of those classes is the Paladin, but that is exclusive to players who pre-order the expansion. It was also previously announced that the update would overhaul skill trees, with the development team joking back in February that they were putting an end to players calling the existing skill tree system “skill twigs.”

Every class will receive skill tree updates, including over 40 choices and more than 80 additional options. Players who purchased the Lord of Hatred DLC will also have 20 extra transformative skill options.

Learn more about the upcoming expansion here.

What do you think about the possibility of Diablo 4’s latest expansion coming to the Nintendo Switch family of consoles? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Yerba Buena Preview: “We Have Portal at Home”
item.content:encoded:
Apparently the settlement that would become the city of San Francisco was once called Yerba Buena, which is where this quirky physics platformer got its name. Set in an alternate reality 1970’s version of the City by the Bay, my 90-minute demo had me rushing to rescue a friend, discovering the first seeds of a government conspiracy, and scratching my head over a lot of the writing decisions.

On the plus side, the art in Yerba Buena is quite charming. It reminds me a bit of some of Telltale's older games, like it was torn out of the pages of a graphic novel. The characters are richly designed with clothing and hairstyles that ground everything in the ‘70s. The colorful streets capture an almost mythological ideal of SF, a city I lived in for years and still feel very fondly about. And the jaunty soundtrack is fun, too. The writing doesn't exactly complete the illusion, though.

The main character is Barb, an out-of-work Midwest transplant who’s likeable enough. But nothing in Yerba Buena's plot seems to flow naturally from what came before it. There are simply fixed moments it wants to get to and it will make any excuse to jump from where we are to where it wants to be, even if it requires bizarre leaps of logic or characters coming to really weird conclusions. The whole tension of the first section hinges on the fact that the SFPD have decided the life of one of Barb's friends who was taken hostage by bikers isn't important, and they're going to storm the building to get the perp even if it means sacrificing a civilian.

The Oscillator is a technomagical device that allows you, at first, to copy the movement of one object and apply it to another.“

Look, I've had my fair share of negative interactions with the police, but no hostage negotiation I've ever heard of amounted to, "Tough luck, he shouldn't have been taken hostage!" This leads Barb to have to mount a one-woman vigilante rescue mission involving time travel and physics puzzles. And that's where we get into the main gameplay mechanic: The Oscillator.

In essence, Yerba Buena seems like it kind of wants to be Portal. The Oscillator is a technomagical device that allows you, at first, to copy the movement of one object and apply it to another. There are some genuinely cool applications for this. You can copy a speeding car and then apply it to an entire apartment, causing it to fly across the street with you in it and let you access the rooftops the next block over. Later, it gains the ability to "copy" any vapor and then apply that property to a solid object to allow you or other objects to pass through it.

It's a neat idea, even if there are only two areas to mess around with The Oscillator in the demo aside from a brief training course: a San Francisco city block and a psychedelic amusement park that exists in some kind of pocket dimension? Again, the story is pretty difficult to parse. I found a mix of clever puzzles with a few that I'm not sure were super well thought-out. But the potential of these mechanics at least has me curious to see what else the full game might pull off. I suppose the writing does as well, but in more of an, “unable to look away from a train wreck” sort of way. There's a very, "I went to San Francisco for GDC a couple times and decided I wanted to set a game here" feeling to how German studio Mad About Pandas depicts everything. It's the ‘70s, but there's a major plot point about a tech mogul wanting to ruin a local park by building a giant TV antenna, which seems like it's struggling to take more recent problems and transport them back in time to the chosen era.It gets even weirder when you find out the fugitives who kidnapped your friend were apparently – and I'm not making any of this up – a special biker gang unit in the Vietnam War who were experimented on to give them superpowers like the Avengers or something. You reveal this by discovering a certificate of Court Martial – that looks like an award you would get in school and hang on the wall – with the name of the gang filled in on a blank line. The tone is kind of light-hearted, but this isn't played for laughs. It's not a comedy game. At least, I don't think it's supposed to be?In addition to this, the leader of the gang, Bear, seems to be able to reload a save any time he dies. Literally. You see a menu pop up over his dead body while he selects the option to reset time to an earlier point. Seemingly all of the characters are aware that this is happening, including the ones who aren't in the gang! But they handwave it away as a trivial oddity just like they do everything else, because no one in this universe follows logic resembling our Earth logic.

Yerba Buena Screenshots

Its endearing visual style and relatable main character weren't quite enough to get me excited to play more Yerba Buena given the confusing plot and dismal quality of the writing. The puzzle possibilities of The Oscillator might, though. Especially if we haven't seen all of the tricks it can do yet. You'll be able to see it for yourself on May 26 on PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X|S.

Leana Hafer is a contributing freelancer for IGN with a specialty in RPGs, strategy, horror, and survival games. She has been reviewing video games professionally since 2010 and is one of IGN's most prolific contributors, with more than 100 reviews published. You can also find her work on sites like PC Gamer and PCGamesN.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/yerba-buena-preview-portal-preview

item.title: Assassin’s Creed: Black Flag Remake Reportedly Set for July Release
item.content:encoded:
In March, Ubisoft released the first piece of concept art for Assassin's Creed: Black Flag Resynced, confirming the existence of the remake of the beloved pirate installment of the series, and today we have a release date, July 9. The date was reported by Insider Gaming but hasn't been confirmed by Ubisoft as yet.

In a (now deleted) March blog post from Ubisoft's Jean Guesdon, Head of Content did hint at the rumors of the remake. "Speculation around Assassin's Creed is not new, but it's worth repeating: "Nothing is true. Everything is permitted." Well, except in this case, some whispers have a little more wind in their sails. Keep your spyglass on the horizon. 🦜"

The project's official name, Assassin's Creed: Black Flag Resynced, was seen in December, when it popped up via a European PEGI ratings board listing. That sighting followed a few hints from the original game's lead actor.

"Players can be excited about some remakes,” Ubisoft CEO Yves Guillemot said in June 2024, “which will allow us to revisit some of the games we've created in the past and modernize them; there are worlds in some of our older Assassin's Creed games that are still extremely rich.”

Rachel Weber is the Head of Editorial Development at IGN and an elder millennial. She's been a professional nerd since 2006 when she got her start on Official PlayStation Magazine in the UK, and has since worked for GamesIndustry.Biz, Rolling Stone and GamesRadar. She loves horror, horror movies, horror games, Red Dead Redemption 2, and her Love and Deepspace boyfriends.



https://www.ign.com/articles/assassins-creed-black-flag-remake-reportedly-set-for-july-release

item.title: Altar of Blood Revamp And New Bartering Routes Arrive In Black Desert's Latest Update
item.content:encoded:
Big bartering and trade route updates as well as a long-awaited revamp of Altar of Blood arrive with this week’s Black Desert Online PC update, both of which were announced at the Calpheon Ball. 

In the Altar of Blood, players party up in either premade or matchmade groups of three to defend a big pillar from waves of enemies, with bosses appearing every three waves. There are currently 21 waves, meaning seven bosses, and each enterable level will depend on the player with the lowest AP and waves cleared. 

There are also weekly rewards that are both guaranteed and based on chance: the Resplendent Origin of Dark Hunger, which is guaranteed once per week, and the Resplendent Essence of Devouring, four of which are combined for the Origin item, both among other rewards. 

There are huge updates to the bartering and trade route systems. Some Level 6 and 7 trade routes have been opened, and with it, trade goods can no longer be moved via Transport. The new routes remove the fog from Crow’s Next, with Crow Coin exchanges moved to this island. Plus, low-level trade goods have had their weight reduced to make it easier for captains to get things sailing, and the Barter button interactability distance has been increased by 20%, on top of other changes to pursue. 

Some big UI updates arrive in this update as well, including an improved World Map search and a new Boss Info UI that unifies previously-scattered information about Bosses throughout the game. The full list of patch changes is available as always on the site, including even more additions such as new Shai Donkey Quests and new Koi to capture, and the patch is live now.

The weekly updates continue for Black Desert Online, which celebrates 10 years in the western market this year.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/altar-of-blood-revamp-and-new-bartering-routes-arrive-in-black-deserts-latest-update-2000137822

item.title: Union Wins Settlement for Ubisoft Halifax Laid-Off Staff
item.content:encoded:
The laid-off workers from Ubisoft’s Halifax branch have voted in favor of a settlement with the company.

In January, it was reported that the CWA Canada union, representing 61 of the 71 employees who lost their jobs when Ubisoft Halifax was shut down, had filed a legal complaint. The studio closed days after the announcement that 61 of its employees had joined the Game & Media Workers Guild of Canada, Local 30111.

CWA Canada Secures Deal for Ubisoft Halifax Workers

In a new announcement, the union revealed that its members voted overwhelmingly in favor of the settlement with Ubisoft.

The union negotiated the deal for its members. There is also a statement from a former Ubisoft employee expressing gratitude to the union for negotiating the package for the impacted staffers. There’s also a statement from the union’s president.

“The success of the negotiation was directly attributable to the solidarity our members showed, their resolve in holding Ubisoft to account, the collecting and sharing of information, and remaining steadfast in advocating for their rights as unionized workers,” CWA Canada President Carmel Smyth said. “Our union is very proud to have provided these workers with the help of our staff and lawyers to work through this difficult time.”

The CWA Canada President also noted that Ubisoft respected the union and acted professionally, but that negotiations weren’t easy.

For more on Ubisoft’s recent financial struggles, check out the canceled projects that resulted from these closures.

Insider Gaming will continue to provide the latest news on all things gaming.

How do you feel about this announcement? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: MapleStory Classic World CBT Preview
item.content:encoded:
I played the MapleStory Classic World CBT as a non-nostalgic MMO player, here's what I found.

MapleStory Classic World's first closed beta test just went live - it runs from April 16th to April 23rd. This is a pre-Big Bang classic server for MapleStory, built into the existing GMS client, and it is being directed by Nemi, one of the original designers from the Wizet team back in 2001. So the person who helped build this game over 20 years ago is the one leading the project, which I think is pretty cool. I got a few hours into the CBT and I want to share what I experienced and what I think. 

What Is MapleStory Classic World?

So for context, Nexon announced Classic World back in April 2025 during the 20th anniversary of GMS, and they've been doing tests ever since. They had a private playtest in June 2025 with 30 players picked out of over 18,000 applications. Then they did MapleCon in LA, a PC café test with like 1,900 participants, and now this closed beta test is the first time people could play from home worldwide. The full launch is estimated somewhere around Q3 to Q4 2026, but Nexon hasn't confirmed a date yet. The dev lead Inkwell has been calling it a "parallel MapleStory," like a timeline where the Big Bang update never happened. He literally used a Spider-Verse analogy to explain the concept.

Now, this is not the MapleStory you know if you've been playing the modern version. The modern game has over 50 classes, level cap of 300, and like a dozen layered progression systems - Star Force, Cubes, Potentials, Arcane Force, Legion, all of that stuff. In principle, Classic World will strip all of that out completely. You only have four Explorer classes at launch: Warrior, Mage, Thief and Archer. No Pirates, no Cygnus Knights, none of the post-Big Bang classes. You manually distribute your stat points every level. You manually assign your skill points. The damage cap is 199,999 instead of the billions you see in live, and the exp curve is the original pre-Big Bang curve, which means reaching level 50 could take you weeks and hitting level 70 was considered a real achievement back in the day. The economy runs on the Free Market, where players set up physical shops in the game world - no Auction House, no Meso Market. And when you die, you lose experience, so boss fights actually feel risky.

They also added some new stuff though, this isn't a pure 1-to-1 recreation. There's a brand new zone called Forgotten Hollow for levels 39 to 59, with 10 new monsters that never existed in any version of MapleStory ever. They built a full crafting system with 6 professions and 342 recipes, which is apparently the deepest non-combat system ever added to the classic engine. They fixed the widescreen support, the old Archer and Assassin melee bug when enemies got too close, boss spawn timers got shortened, and HP Washing is gone - Inkwell straight up said it was unfair and essentially a bug.

The Early Game Experience

Alright, so let me walk you through what I actually played. You drop into Maple Road and the game introduces you to a bunch of NPCs right away. Robin explains how death works, how to move around, and what to do to pick a job. And then you're off killing your first little snails. A little further along you meet Sam, who asks you to go kill more snails, which is about as classic MMO as it gets. And then there's Mai, who wants you to kill even more snails, but blue and red this time. It's all part of the early training.

Then it's the orange snails - bigger, stronger, rarer. And at the same time, another NPC, Maria, asks you to deliver a letter to the elder of Amherst, which is apparently the nearest town. Delivering that letter to Lucas gave me my first equippable item, so that was a nice little reward for doing the side quest. The orange mushrooms were the biggest challenge in that early training, and then the next step is to hit level 10 to choose your job, which is basically your class.

After those first steps on Maple Island, an NPC named Rain tells you to take a boat from South Perry to Victoria Island, which is the first real zone of the game. I was level 7 at that point. And at South Perry, I ran into Biggs, who wanted an orange shell and ten blue shells - kind of a continuation of the earlier quests - and he gave me a better weapon, a razor. So the progression there felt smooth; you're constantly getting little upgrades as you go.

And honestly, walking around MapleStory Classic World is just really pleasant. The world is colorful, animated, charming. There's a distinct visual identity that's kind of childlike and fun, and every single NPC has their own dialogue that gives you information about the world, tells you what you might find in certain towns, what's worth checking out. The NPCs themselves are all recognizable - I'm thinking of Mister Kim the banker. Dude has his own completely unique look compared to every other NPC. I thought that was cool. You can tell the game has soul.

Once on Victoria Island, after following Olaf's training and doing some more side quests that were pretty similar - kill slimes, kill pigs, you know the drill - I finally hit level 10, which meant visiting Phil at Lith Harbor who directed me toward Perion to officially become a Warrior!

Now, I was about to write that the game can be pretty frustrating because it gives you potions but you don't loot many, and you need to use them wisely. And then I realized that, like a true Warrior, I had not spent a single talent point this whole time - which probably explains why those pigs were so tough. My bad, that one's on me.

First Impressions After A Few Hours

I would have loved to spend more time in the post-opening phase of this CBT. But the few hours I got were genuinely enjoyable. The world, like I said, is colorful, funny. The NPCs, the mobs, everything has a distinct visual identity, and you can feel the charm. The game has character.

However, the first ten levels felt repetitive on the combat front. Until you pick your job at level 10, you're essentially limited to a single melee attack. There is a shell throw ability from the beginner skills, but it's fairly anecdotal. You also get a speed buff and a regen buff, which are nice to have, but in terms of actual active combat abilities, you're stuck with one button for a while. Things do open up once you hit level 10 and choose your class. As a Warrior, I unlocked Power Strike and then Slash Blast right away at level 11, giving me a proper single-target hit and a small AoE. That felt noticeably better. But it still took me roughly three hours to get there, and that's a good chunk of early gameplay where the combat doesn't really shine.

The platforming side of things is actually satisfying. Navigating the maps, jumping between platforms, positioning yourself against mobs - that part feels good and makes the 2D side-scrolling format work well. I just felt like I needed more tools to play with while doing it. Coming from games where you're actively dodging, comboing or managing cooldowns, the moment-to-moment gameplay here felt a bit flat to me.

Now, I think for longtime MapleStory fans, that simplicity might actually be part of the appeal. This is how the game was, and that's what they're coming back for.

Monetization

As far as monetization goes, we obviously didn't get the real prices in the cash shop during the CBT. They gave us 5,000 Leaf Points and every item cost 100 Leaf Points, so I'm pretty sure they deliberately hid the real pricing for this test. As for what's actually in the shop, I didn't see anything exceptionally pay to win. You've got your standard pets that can pick up your loot, and you can enhance them with spells so they automatically move to loot and auto-restore your HP and mana through potions - which you can't do without going through the cash shop. So there will be regular purchases on that front if you want to stay competitive, since pets have a limited lifespan.

Now, Nexon did say at MapleCon that the cash shop would be cosmetic only - no EXP coupons, no Miracle Cubes, no upgrade scrolls for real money. And honestly, the community is watching this super closely, because Nexon's track record with pay to win in modern MapleStory is not great. Inkwell's answer about Gachapon being kind of vague didn't help either. A lot of players are saying MapleStory Classic deserves a chance, but not blind loyalty, which I think is fair.

Should You Try It?

The game is set to come out later this year, for free, so it's something I can only recommend you go try for yourselves when it launches. I think the players who will stick with MapleStory Classic World are either huge MapleStory nostalgics who want to relive the origins of the game, or they're MMO genre curious people like me who want to explore a pillar of the genre. 

They've also taken some liberties with Classic World - the Forgotten Hollow zone I mentioned earlier is entirely new content. So it's not out of the question that future updates could add more original content, or even rework existing stuff. Either way, I'm genuinely curious to go further in my MapleStory adventure when it launches! 

Emilien Lecoffre

Emilien 'Nephistos' has been immersed in MMOs since his early teens on Dofus. Over the past years, he also has been sharing his insights on JeuxOnline, a major French-language MMO sites. While he keeps a keen eye on all market offerings, his true passion lies in RvR and mass PvP.



https://www.mmorpg.com/previews/maplestory-classic-world-cbt-preview-2000137829

item.title: Phasmophobia is getting an Alan Wake 2 crossover event next month, which would be cool if it wasn't one time only
item.content:encoded:
To be a live service game, in any shape or form, is to seemingly make some kind of agreement to eventually make some kind of licensing deal. And it is happening again. This time with Phasmophobia and, to its credit, the quite thematically appropriate Alan Wake 2, which is coming to the co-op horror game as part of a limited-time event.

How limited-time you ask? Well, it kicks off on May 12th, next month, and runs until June 2nd, so very limited. Not only is it for such a short period of time, but according to the announcement post over on Steam this is a one time event, so if you miss it, you're out of luck. Certainly one of the more annoying aspects of Phasmophobia as even events of the developer's own making only come at certain points in the year, and sometimes held for just the one occasion. Details beyond what is shown off in the trailer are incredibly minimal, only sharing that you'll "unravel a new chapter from Alan Wake, unlock in-game rewards and more." Firmer details will come closer to the start date.

The trailer itself gives a smidge more away, showing off a bunch of locales that have been given an Alan Wake 2 themed makeover. Most obvious of the bunch is Mr. Wake's writing desk, but for the most part it's just a bunch of eerie, sort-of trippy, vaguely Alan Wake cinematography inspired shots that don't make it all that clear as to what you'll actually be doing. At least it sounds like they got Alan Wake's voice Matthew Porretta to do the voice over for the trailer! That's fun.

Apparently Remedy directly collaborated with Kinetic Games in making the crossover, making it entirely possible that there will be some semblance of canonicity to be found here. I personally hope not. As much as I like the Remedy Connected Universe, I'd like it to stay contained. In any case, make sure you hop on to Phasmophobia May 12th if you hate missing events or like stories about writers who kind of suck ass a little bit.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/phasmophobia-is-getting-an-alan-wake-2-crossover-event-next-month-which-would-be-cool-if-it-wasnt-one-time-only

item.title: Here's Everything in Next Week's World of Warcraft 12.0.5 Update
item.content:encoded:
Christina Gonzalez Updated: Apr 16, 2026 5:30 PM Posted: Apr 16, 2026 5:30 PM ET
Category: News 0

Time to continue Midnight's story, take on more of the Void forces and keep working to push them back, and to take a moment to hide as a piece of furniture in the lightest form of PvP in World of Warcraft's next update. While the update isn't out just yet (that's coming April 21st), the update notes have just been released. In case you want to know how the Voidforge evolves or how those Decor Duels actually work.

You can read on for everything to know about the update. We shared an overview recently of 12.05., but now you can read on below to find out the full details of all of the new content, features, fixes, and improvements.

Originally Posted by Blizzard(Blue Tracker)

Engage in new activities, experience more of the story in Midnight, and earn more loot as the efforts to push back the forces of the Void and their agents continue. You’ll undertake new Void Assaults, disrupt powerful Ritual Sites, get a boost with Voidforge, group up for Decor Duels, and more.

STAVE OFF VOID ASSAULTS

The forces of the Void are vying for position and control amongst themselves in a bid for more power. As they seek every opportunity they can, attacks begin in the Eversong Woods and Zul’Aman zones. [LEARN MORE]

Void Strike

  • Void Strikes take part in Eversong Woods or Zul’Aman on a weekly rotation. Once completed, a new one will begin in another part of the zone and continue to rotate through the zones.

Void Incursion

  • These larger scenarios occur when enough Void Strikes have been completed in the zones and will take the might of many of Azeroth’s champions to defeat them.

DISRUPT RITUAL SITES

Put a stop to rituals meant to empower the ambitions of power-hungry naga and Twilight’s Blade cultists in small one to five player instances. As you take on greater challenges, you’ll be able to receive greater rewards.

In Ritual Sites, you’ll choose some of the challenges you’ll face as you climb through the tiers. Players will also unlock Great Vault rewards for the World content row alongside Delves and Prey.

Players will earn Field Accolades from both Void Assaults and Ritual Sites as currency they can use to purchase Champion and Heroic quality gear and rewards. [LEARN MORE]

GET A LEG UP WITH THE VOIDFORGE

The Voidforge will provide new opportunities for players in Midnight to take their game further. You’ll help aid domanaar Decimus build a Voidforge to begin transmuting Nebulous Voidcores, which are transmutable into powerful equipment upon defeating powerful foes in Midnight Season 1 raids or completing Mythic+ dungeons, and Bountiful Delves and Prey Hunts on Nightmare difficulty.

After unlocking the Voidforge, Decimus will require additional assistance to craft the Ascendant Nilhammer, allowing players to upgrade their weapons and trinkets to new heights by collecting Ascendant Voidcores.

Nebulous Voidcore

Players can use Nebulous Voidcores to more easily obtain specific equipment whether they're chasing best-in-slot items on their main character or looking to quickly gear up their alternate characters later in the season. 

Upon defeating a Midnight Season 1 raid boss or completing a Mythic+ Dungeon, Bountiful Delve, or Prey Hunt on Nightmare difficulty, players may choose to spend their Nebulous Voidcores to receive a random item appropriate for their Loot Specialization from that activity. After receiving an item in this way, it is removed from that loot pool until all eligible items have been obtained on a per-difficulty basis. For example, receiving the Ranger-Captain's Iridescent Insignia trinket from the Crown of the Cosmos encounter in the Voidspire on Normal difficulty will not remove it from consideration on Heroic or Mythic difficulty. The item level of Mythic+ rewards from Nebulous Voidcores is equivalent to Great Vault rewards from that Keystone level, i.e., Myth 1/6 at Keystone level +10 and above.

Decimus will offer Nebulous Voidcores to the player in exchange for a choice of valuable resources including gold, Voidlight Marl, and Veteran Dawncrests. The total number of Nebulous Voidcores available to a character increases by two every week for the remainder of the season. Due to the smaller loot pools and the exceptional power of raid items, these rewards will require two Nebulous Voidcores upon defeating a raid boss. Other endgame content, such as Mythic+, will cost only one. As a result, acquiring a particularly desired trinket, weapon, or ring is likely to require a similar total of Nebulous Voidcores regardless of source. For example, a player chasing a trinket from a raid boss with three total items matching their Loot Specialization is likely to receive that trinket in the same amount of time it would take to acquire a trinket from a Mythic+ dungeon with six total items.

Players can also purchase a Nebulous Voidcore from Vaultkeeper Elysa for six Thalassian Tokens of Merit. Nebulous Voidcores acquired in this way do not count against the weekly increasing cap.

Ascendant Voidcore

Following the Voidforge's construction, Decimus will ask players to assist him in crafting the Ascendant Nilhammer, a powerful tool capable of manipulating Ascendant Voidcores to empower worthy equipment. Upon completing this warband-wide journey, players may begin acquiring Ascendant Voidcores from Midnight Season 1 endgame content including raids, Mythic+ dungeons, Bountiful Delves, and Nightmare difficulty Prey Hunts.

Consuming an Ascendant Voidcore infuses an eligible weapon or trinket with additional power to increase its item level. Only fully upgraded Hero, Myth and maximum quality Radiance Crafted weapons and trinkets are eligible to become Ascendant Voidforged.

HIDE OR SEEK WITH DECOR DUELS

Amidst all the danger that swirls above Azeroth, there’s something to be said about having a little fun. Players can join in a team vs team hide-and-seek style game taking place in Silvermoon City through the Group Finder Tool (PvP tab) to either hide as various Housing decor items or seek out those who have donned the guise of an item. You’ll earn new rewards, including a mount, toys, and Housing decor, through this bite-sized event. [LEARN MORE]

NEW ACTIVITY: ABYSS ANGLERS

Dive deep into the shimmering waters off Zul'Aman's rugged coast, where schools of exotic fish swarm just beneath the surface in a new repeatable event—Abyss Anglers. Team up to spear as many as you can and rack up points to unlock fishing rewards and upgrades to your diver gear, allowing you to venture deeper into the abyss where the greatest treasures await.

Seek out Depthdiver Jeju to suit up and enter the Depths. Zip through whirlpools, catch creatures with your harpoon, and discover treasures amidst the underwater steam vents.

Prove your mastery to earn dive equipment upgrades and cosmetics.

  • Fixed an issue where players who already owned the Sun Darter Hatchling pet were not receiving Mind-Seeker progress and were required to loot the pet again.

DUNGEONS AND RAIDS

  • MYTHIC+
    • New Feature: Lindormi's Glow – Lindormi's Glow highlights select enemy forces even when the Lindormi's Guidance affix is inactive. Defeating all highlighted enemies will complete the enemy forces requirement for a Keystone Dungeon.
      • Any player whose class supports a tank role specialization can choose to use Lindormi's Glow by speaking with Lindormi near the The Timeways portal or at the end of a Keystone Dungeon.
      • Lindormi's Glow will activate in a Keystone Dungeon if the party's tank has chosen to use this feature.
      • Players can disable Lindormi's Glow by speaking with Lindormi again.
    • New Achievement: Midnight Keystone Myth: Season One – Attain a Mythic+ Rating of at least 3400 during Midnight Season 1. Rewards a Timelost Saddle that can be exchanged with Lindormi for a mount of your choice. Mounts include: A variety of new mounts, and Keystone Master and Keystone Legend mounts from previous seasons.
      • Developers' notes: We want to give dedicated Mythic+ players something that continues to feel rewarding over time, while also creating a path to revisit some older rewards you may have missed along the way. Keystone Myth will launch with a 3400 rating requirement, but we do not expect that threshold to remain fixed forever. Mythic+ evolves from season to season, and we want this achievement to continue to represent a similar level of difficulty and prestige each time. Because of that, we may adjust the rating requirement in future seasons.
    • New Achievement: Umbral Champion: Midnight Season One – End Midnight Mythic+ Season 1 with a Mythic+ Rating in the top 1% of all players in your region. Rewards a new exclusive mount.
  • MARCH ON QUEL'DANAS
    • Midnight Falls
      • Dusk Crystal health reduced by 10% on Mythic difficulty.
      • Glimmering damage reduced by 10% on Mythic difficulty.
      • Shattered Sky damage reduced by 10% on Mythic difficulty.

HOUSING

  • You can now click Decor links in chat to open the Decor Catalog. Shift+Clicking Decor will also link it in chat.
  • If a guild member currently in the guild neighborhood is kicked or leaves the guild, they will now be teleported out of the guild neighborhood during their eviction. Their eviction notice should be visible after teleporting.
  • Implemented new budget icons for House Upgrades.
  • Decor and Houses now fade in, rather than simply popping in at distance.
  • Added a friendlier message when you try to enter a House that you are not allowed in.
  • Updated the chat message when acquiring Decor to include links to the Decor in the Catalog
  • Your Dyed versions of the same Decor are now shown on one card instead of multiple cards in the Decor Catalog, with the ability to cycle between your owned variants in the preview window.
  • In the Cornerstone pane, updated the confirmation dialogs for Buy and Move to include the plot number, Neighborhood, and price.
  • Added a new tutorial helptip for how to switch Endeavors. Added a reminder in the inactive Endeavor's page that only one Endeavor can be contributed to at a time.
  • Added a tooltip for the Endeavors completion reward that displays the House XP and Community Coupons earned.
  • HOUSE EXTERIOR EDITOR IMPROVEMENTS:
    • Added a popup to warn about Decor attached to your House when swapping types.
    • Added a hide/show Decor toggle while editing your House Exterior.
    • We now prevent position and rotation from resetting when swapping exterior House types.
    • Updated Exterior House Editor button to use a unique button icon.

ITEMS

  • Fixed an issue where Gloom-Spattered Dreadscale absorb shield value could be incorrectly calculated when damaging multiple enemies.

PLAYER VERSUS PLAYER

  • MONK
    • Mistweaver
      • Mana Tea now restores 50% less mana in PvP combat.
      • Life Cocoon's absorb amount reduced by 25% in PvP combat.
      • Chrysalis reduces the cooldown of Life Cocoon by 30 seconds in PvP combat (was 45 seconds).
      • Zen Spheres can now be applied by Rising Sun Kick and Rushing Wind Kick.

PROFESSIONS

  • Cooking and vendor refreshments now restore health and mana based on a percentage, rather than a fixed amount.
  • The Deftness stat now scales at a slower rate for Dragonflight, Khaz Algar, and Midnight professions.
    • Developers' notes: As a side effect of squishing stats and item levels, Deftness scaled disproportionately faster than other professions stats, making it trivial to reach 100%+ Deftness in Dragonflight and Khaz Algar professions. This change brings Deftness back in line to expected values.

USER INTERFACE AND ACCESSIBILITY

  • Now you can shift click a feature's checkbox in Edit Mode to enable the feature directly. This applies to features including Cooldown Manager, Damage Meter, Boss Warnings, External Defensives, and Loot Window. 
  • The default size of raid frames and arena frames has been increased.
  • Added a slider to change the size of the Big Defensive icon on raid frames.
  • Added the ability to invite cross faction players to a party from the Recent Allies window.
  • Damage Meter windows now have a minimize/maximize button, leaving only the header bar when minimized.
  • There is a new icon for "stay a while and listen" NPCs.
  • Fixed an issue where the Boss Timeline was shown for encounters with no timeline events.
  • Fixed an issue that could disrupt auto-loot functionality in some situations.
  • TRANSMOGRIFICATION
    • New weapon sheathing options have been added, including an option to hide weapons when sheathed.
    • Added a Sheathe Weapon toggle to the transmog preview pane.
    • Added a /outfit command to change outfits. Use /outfit x, with x being the outfit's order number in the transmogrification pane. Using /outfit with no number or /outfit x with that outfit already equipped will clear all transmogs instead (use /outfit !x to avoid this behavior). Trial of Style outfit uses /outfit 0.
    • Two Situations have been added: Weather and Time of Day.
    • The Sets and Custom Sets section now have a more detailed tooltip that shows the collection status of the set and its appearances.
    • When there is an outfit discount in effect, the cost is now green with a tooltip showing the source of the discount.
    • Added a glow to the "Save Outfit" button when there are pending changes.
    • Fixed an issue where certain items with special visual effects, such as Forest Lord's Antlers, would be displayed incorrectly when applied as a transmogrification.

To view all content update notes, click here.

For World of Warcraft customer support, please visit our Support Site or our Customer Support discussion forum. If you’ve found a bug, please let us know about it in our Bug Report Forum.

Christina Gonzalez

Christina is MMORPG.COM’s News Editor and a contributor since 2011. Always a fan of great community and wondering if the same sort of magic that was her first guild exists anymore.

item.title: Disney Lorcana TCG: Collection Starter Set - Stitch Edition Has One of the Coolest Cards and It's Also Great for Beginners
item.content:encoded:
If you are new to Disney Lorcana, are looking to learn more and build your collection, or simply want one of the coolest cards I've seen yet from the trading card game, I strongly recommend this Disney Lorcana TCG: Collection Starter Set - Stitch Edition after getting my hands on it earlier this month.

This Stitch-themed starter set is part of the larger Winterspell expansion that was recently released, and it comes complete with a Stitch - Rock Star card portfolio, four Winterspell booster packs, a very handy collector's guide, and a "Glimmer Foil" Stitch - Carefree Snowboarder Promo Card.

Disney Lorcana TCG: Collection Starter Set - Stitch Edition - Includes 4 Winterspell Booster Packs

I want to start with the card because it is GORGEOUS. Not only does it feature some amazing art from Federico Maria Cugliari of Stitch having the time of his life snowboarding, but it is all gold and shimmers with the colors of the rainbow in the proper light.

It has become a new showpiece in my collection, and it really stands out from the other cards in a way that makes it feel special and rare. It's also just a great decision by the Lorcana team as Stitch is more popular than ever these days with his box-office smash hit live-action film and a sequel on the way.

The Stitch card also looks great in the Stitch - Rock Star card portfolio, which can hold up to 80 cards and keeps them safe and secure.

Speaking of the cards, the set comes with four Winterspell booster packs. I didn't have the greatest luck, but I did manage to snag a Legendary Tod - Knows All the Tricks from The Fox and the Hound and a Rare 'Do You Want to Build a Snowman?,' which we were able to exclusively reveal prior to the launch of Winterspell.

All in all, the four booster packs contained 48 cards, 25 of which were common (52.08%). 14 were Uncommon (29.16%), eight were Rare (16.66%), one was Legendary (2.08%), and four of them were Holo cards (8.33%).

In addition to everything else, this starter set includes a small Collector's Guide. Sure, you could find all this info online, but it's nice to have this small card-sized booklet that breaks down what you need to know about Disney Lorcana.

Disney Lorcana TCG: Collection Starter Set - Stitch Edition

This guide helps you understand each part of a card, how to build your collection and keep it safe, what the rarity levels are, how you can see all your cards in a digital library, and more.

Overall, I highly recommend this Starter Set. At a $30 MSRP, it's roughly $5-$6 more than buying a booster pack on its own. That means, instead of one more pack, you can guarantee a really great Stitch card, a card portfolio to match, and a bit of info on how to become a better player/collector.

There is so much more planned for Lorcana this year, including the upcoming Wilds Unknown set that will introduce Pixar, Toy Story, and The Incredibles into the TCG for the first time ever.

For more, check out our impressions of Winterspell's Illuminier's Trove, the top chase cards for Winterspell and Fabled and what else to expect from Lorcana in 2026.

Adam Bankhurst is a writer for IGN. You can follow him on X/Twitter @AdamBankhurst, Instagram, and TikTok, and listen to his show, Talking Disney Magic.

item.title: Once Human's PvP Mode Will Get 'Casual' and 'Hardcore' Mode, Plus New Enemies and Items
item.content:encoded:
The apocalyptic fantasy survival game Once Human will bring major changes to its PvP mode, RaidZone, in its upcoming biweekly update, making it more vastly approachable and adding more content. 

The most prominent change coming is the new designated markers for Once Human’s RaidZone servers, which not only change how players self-sort, but also affect rulesets. These will be “Casual,” “Standard” and “Hardcore”

The new “casual” mode will have 14-day and 28-day durations, where players only drop weapons and gear, the raid windows are a frequent 4 hours, and there’ll be a degree of territory protection. In short, there’s a little more room to breathe, but there are still stakes once you get to exploring. 

Things ramp up with the 7-day Standard mode, as everything drops on death, raids are more competitive with 8-hour windows, and maintenance will require materials. Hardcore mode turns up the raid window to a cutthroat 24 hours each. 

Beyond these divisions, there are some content additions to keep things interesting. The new Morphic Echoite enemy is exactly what it sounds like: an Echoite chest that’s actually a mimic that’ll attack on approach. There’s a new advanced underground stronghold that’s described as a “high-risk, high-reward objective” with stronger enemies to tackle. 

A few new items are available to grab in RaidZone, and a few are teased in the post. The Rosetta Bio-Gel can be thrown on the ground to mess up enemies, the Airtight Diving Suit lets players linger underwater, and the Dense Energy Device appears to give critically wounded players a boost back to health (though it’s implied there are more effects). Or, if you’re lucky, you can just outright pick up a Giant Crate if you don’t think you have enough storage at home. 

The full Once Human update post also describes “targeted upgrades to our anti-cheat algorithms,” both for combat and general player behavior.

These changes will presumably arrive in the twice-monthly updates that Once Human usually brings. Last week, Starry Studio launched a major overhaul to the “Tech” system that’s tied to player progression, and a PvE update with more enemies and a Deviant came not long before that.
